message.content:
I need to verify which exact phenotype terms from the term bank appear in CYP2C9-relevant papers. The relevant papers are all about warfarin dosing with CYP2C9 variants.

Please run these specific searches:

1. grep -rli "over-anticoagulation" /app/papers/*.md
2. grep -rli "time in therapeutic range" /app/papers/*.md
3. grep -rli "time above therapeutic range" /app/papers/*.md
4. grep -rli "time to therapeutic inr\|time to therapeutic INR" /app/papers/*.md
5. grep -rli "dose reduction" /app/papers/*.md
6. grep -rli "hemorrhage" /app/papers/*.md
7. grep -rli "thromboembolism\b" /app/papers/*.md
8. grep -rli "venous thromboembolism" /app/papers/*.md
9. grep -rli "venous thrombosis\b" /app/papers/*.md
10. grep -rli "atrial fibrillation" /app/papers/*.md
11. grep -rli "stroke\b" /app/papers/*.md
12. grep -rli "heart valve replacement\|heart-valve replacement" /app/papers/*.md
13. grep -rli "thrombotic disease" /app/papers/*.md
14. grep -rli "death\b" /app/papers/*.md
15. grep -rli "cardiovascular disease" /app/papers/*.md
16. grep -rli "coronary artery disease" /app/papers/*.md
17. grep -rli "coronary disease\b" /app/papers/*.md
18. grep -rli "heart diseases\b\|heart disease\b" /app/papers/*.md
19. grep -rli "drug toxicity" /app/papers/*.md
20. grep -rli "adverse events\b\|adverse drug" /app/papers/*.md
21. grep -rli "drug interaction\b" /app/papers/*.md

For each, just show the matching file names.

Also:
22. To confirm "time above therapeutic range" - exact search:
grep -rn "time above therapeutic" /app/papers/*.md | head -5

23. Check if death appears in CYP2C9-relevant papers:
grep -n "death" /app/papers/PMC5818817.md | head -10

content.text: I'll run all these searches in parallel for efficiency.

input.command: grep -rli "time to therapeutic inr\|time to therapeutic INR" /app/papers/*.md 2>/dev/null

input.description: Search 22: confirm exact "time above therapeutic" with line context

content.content:
18:In this multicenter randomized clinical trial that included 1650 patients undergoing elective hip or knee arthroplasty, genotype-guided warfarin dosing compared with clinically guided warfarin dosing reduced the rate of a composite of major bleeding, international normalized ratio of 4 or greater, venous thromboembolism, or death from 14.7% to 10.8%.
29:In this multicenter randomized clinical trial that included 1650 patients undergoing elective hip or knee arthroplasty, genotype-guided warfarin dosing compared with clinically guided warfarin dosing reduced the rate of a composite of major bleeding, international normalized ratio of 4 or greater, venous thromboembolism, or death from 14.7% to 10.8%.
53:The primary end point was the composite of major bleeding, INR of 4 or greater, venous thromboembolism, or death. Patients underwent a screening lower-extremity duplex ultrasound approximately 1 month after arthroplasty.
57:Among 1650 randomized patients (mean age, 72.1 years [SD, 5.4 years]; 63.6% women; 91.0% white), 1597 (96.8%) received at least 1 dose of warfarin therapy and completed the trial (n = 808 in genotype-guided group vs n = 789 in clinically guided group). A total of 87 patients (10.8%) in the genotype-guided group vs 116 patients (14.7%) in the clinically guided warfarin dosing group met at least 1 of the end points (absolute difference, 3.9% [95% CI, 0.7%-7.2%], *P* = .02; relative rate [RR], 0.73 [95% CI, 0.56-0.95]). The numbers of individual events in the genotype-guided group vs the clinically guided group were 2 vs 8 for major bleeding (RR, 0.24; 95% CI, 0.05-1.15), 56 vs 77 for INR of 4 or greater (RR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.51-0.99), 33 vs 38 for venous thromboembolism (RR, 0.85; 95% CI, 0.54-1.34), and there were no deaths.
61:Among patients undergoing elective hip or knee arthroplasty and treated with perioperative warfarin, genotype-guided warfarin dosing, compared with clinically guided dosing, reduced the combined risk of major bleeding, INR of 4 or greater, venous thromboembolism, or death. Further research is needed to determine the cost-effectiveness of personalized warfarin dosing.
103:The primary outcome of the study was a composite of the following adverse events: major bleeding within 30 days, INR of 4 or greater within 30 days, death within 30 days, and symptomatic or asymptomatic VTE confirmed by objective testing within 60 days of arthroplasty. *Major bleeding* was defined as (1) bleeding into a critical area (intracranial, epidural, intraocular, pericardial, or retroperitoneal), (2) overt bleeding that resulted in death, (3) a hematoma requiring a return to the operating room, (4) a decrease in hemoglobin level of 2 g/dL or greater, (5) a transfusion of 2 or more units of blood, or (6) hemodynamic changes requiring a transfusion of 1 or more units of blood. Bleeding that did not meet the major bleeding definition was further subclassified as nonmajor clinically relevant bleeding or minor bleeding that was not significant using the definition from prior anticoagulant trials.^7,23,24^
306:Second, the 3.9% absolute reduction in the primary outcome (death, major bleeding event, INR ≥4, or VTE) was primarily related to differences in rates of INR of 4 or greater ([Figure 2](#joi170100f2)). The 1.4% reduction in symptomatic major clinical adverse events (major bleeding, symptomatic DVT, or pulmonary embolism) did not achieve independent statistical significance (*P* = .051). Likewise, the risk of an INR exceeding the target INR by 1.5 or greater was not significantly reduced during the 90 days of follow-up (*P* = .08). Third, in this multicenter trial, most participants were enrolled at high-volume academic medical centers, which may limit generalizability. However, genotype-guided warfarin dosing may be more beneficial at low-volume hospitals, which may have higher rates of adverse events.^30,31^
322:Among patients undergoing elective hip or knee arthroplasty and treated with perioperative warfarin, genotype-guided warfarin dosing, compared with clinically guided dosing, reduced the combined risk of major bleeding, INR of 4 or greater, venous thromboembolism, or death. Further research is needed to determine the cost-effectiveness of personalized warfarin dosing.

**22. Exact "time above therapeutic" confirmation** — No matches. The term "time above therapeutic range" does not appear in any paper.

**23. "death" in /app/papers/PMC5818817.md** — Confirmed present. PMC5818817 uses "death" as part of its primary composite endpoint (major bleeding, INR >= 4, venous thromboembolism, or death). Notably, there were zero actual deaths in the trial ("no deaths" occurred in either arm), but the term is used throughout as a defined endpoint.

---

**Key summary observations:**

- "time above therapeutic range" (search 3) and "thrombotic disease" (search 13) and "coronary disease" (search 17) — zero matches across all papers.
- "time in therapeutic range" appears in only 2 papers; "time to therapeutic INR" also in only 2 papers.
- "heart valve replacement" appears in only 1 paper (PMC5862636.md).
- "over-anticoagulation" is surprisingly well-represented (13 papers), making it a valid phenotype term for this corpus.
- "atrial fibrillation" is the most broadly present clinical context term (35 papers).
- "death" matches only 5 papers, and in PMC5818817 it functions as a trial endpoint label rather than an observed outcome.
